purpose: Govern the future Approved Agent Build Runner and Merge Gate so already human-approved backlog entries can later be built one at a time only when required checks, missing-check evidence, safe-repair boundaries, PR diff review, mergeability, and protected-scope gates are green.
forbiddenAgentBuildCategories: health / child / location / privacy / compliance / legal changes

runtimeAllowedPathsByTaskType.activationRule: Only docs_register_only is enabled for the current report-only runner. ui_copy_safe, route_register_sync, and test_only require an explicit task-specific human approval, matching test plan, PR diff review, and merge-gate evidence before use.
runtimeAllowedPathsByTaskType.blockedPathOverrideRule: If a changed file matches forbiddenPaths, blockedSensitivePaths, blockedSensitiveTopics, or a task type blockedPaths entry, the runner must stop even when another task type allowedPaths entry would otherwise match.
blockedSensitiveTopics: camera, image, video, audio, face, or biometric data
privacy, consent, legal, AGB, Datenschutz, Impressum, or compliance messaging

docs_register_only.description: Existing documentation, register, and governance-validator mode.
requiredChecks: jq empty on every changed project-register/*.json file

ui_copy_safe.description: Small, non-sensitive copy or presentation-only fixes in existing UI surfaces; no behavior, routing, data, reward, wallet, health, privacy, legal, or Unity changes.
scopeLimits: copy, labels, helper text, aria text, or CSS class-level presentation only
no new data collection, business logic, server calls, route changes, dependency changes, or compliance wording changes
no token, NFT, wallet, payment, reward-authority, mission-completion, health, child, location, privacy, legal, or Unity/AR runtime changes
minimumChecks: manual or screenshot review for perceptible UI changes when environment supports it

route_register_sync.description: Synchronize route/page/API inventory registers with existing routes without changing runtime route behavior.
scopeLimits: no route creation, deletion, redirect, middleware, metadata, caching, or navigation behavior changes
minimumChecks: jq empty on every changed project-register/*.json file

test_only.description: Add or adjust tests for already-approved behavior without changing implementation or protected logic.
scopeLimits: test files only; no implementation edits and no dependency changes
tests may document protected behavior but must not create new protected authority or fixtures containing secrets or personal data
minimumChecks: the relevant test command named by the task-specific test plan

requiredBuildChecks: jq empty on every changed project-register/*.json file
any new agent-specific validator created by the build
requiredPostBuildChecks: jq empty on every changed project-register/*.json file
requiredMergeGateChecks: required GitHub checks are success, not missing/pending/failure
required local checks were actually executed and passed
PR diff review reports no protected path findings
auto-merge eligibility reports eligible or explicitly allowed by this policy for docs/register/governance-only approved-agent builds
auto-repair decision reports no unresolved repair required
continuity dependency map updated when needed
requiredCheckEvidenceRules: A PR must not be considered merge-ready if any required check was skipped, not run, missing, unknown, pending or delegated to future CI without evidence.
The runner must record exact commands run and results.
PR body claims must match executed checks.
If a command was not run, merge gate fails.
If GitHub checks are unavailable, merge gate fails unless explicit human override is present.
The PR #109 pattern of checks not executed, should be run later must be classified as not_merge_ready.
missingCheckHandlingRules: The runner must attempt to run missing checks before deciding.
If checks fail for safe docs/register/governance reasons, attempt safe repair.
If checks fail for runtime/protected/high-risk reasons, stop merge and create repair report/plan.
If checks cannot run due environment/tooling issue, mark environment_blocked and do not merge.

safeRepairAttemptRules.allowed: JSON formatting or parse errors in project-register/*.json
missing Work Map pointer for new governance docs/registers
missing TODO Index pointer for new governance docs/registers
missing KI-Fortsetzungs-Prompt in newly added architecture docs
missing expectedPrOutput in an agent-task-queue entry
missing requiredOutputLocations or nextRecommendedTask in continuity-dependency-map.json
missing report-only validator registration in quality-gate.mjs for a newly created governance agent
missing catalog/backlog/proposal metadata for the newly built approved agent
missing progress-log or agent-work-log evidence when changes remain in allowed docs/register paths

safeRepairExecutionRequirements: attempt repair on a new commit in the same PR branch if safe
rerun the failed checks after repair
rerun full required build and post-PR checks after repair
record repair attempt in work log/progress log
do not merge until all required checks pass after repair
unsafeRepairStopRules: reward authority or mission-completion authority changes
failed checks that require product/runtime judgment
mergeEligibilityRules: The runner may only merge approved-agent framework PRs that are docs/register/validator-only and pass every required check.
The runner must not merge if auto-merge eligibility is false due protected path findings.

The runner must not merge if auto-merge eligibility is false only because protected topic words are mentioned as safety boundaries unless PR diff review and protected path checks confirm no protected runtime files changed; in that case policy may allow merge only for report-only docs/register/validator agent-framework PRs.
The runner must not merge if GitHub mergeability is unknown, dirty, blocked, pending or failed.
The runner must not merge if any required check is missing.
The runner must not merge if repair_required remains unresolved.
autoMergeBoundaries: Real auto-merge is limited to approved-agent framework PRs in allowed paths only.
workLogUpdateRules: record missing checks and environment blocks
record safe repair attempts as new PR commits
catalogBacklogUpdateRules: update agent catalog only for the one built approved agent
update approved backlog status only for the one selected entry
do not mark built unless docs/register/validator outputs exist and checks pass
do not change unrelated future-agent statuses
continuityUpdateRules: update continuity-dependency-map.json when the built agent creates a new guardrail, dependency, source-of-truth pointer, or nextRecommendedTask
mark blocked items rather than deleting them
replacementPRRules: do not close PRs by default
replacement PR workflow requires explicit human approval and validated safe replacement policy
never close PR #13 or Unity PRs
stopConditions: GitHub checks unavailable without explicit human override
forbiddenAutoActions: close PRs without explicit validated replacement workflow
